title: Compact diff viewer (text) with character-level highlights
design_brief: Building inline text diff (think Git word-diff, prose review). Pain point: line-level diff hides character-level changes.
ux_reasoning: Character-level diff. Added chars: green bg. Removed: red strikethrough. Same: normal.

design_tokens.colors: Add bg: oklch(0.92 0.1 145 / 0.5). Remove bg: oklch(0.92 0.1 25 / 0.5) strikethrough.

accessibility_considerations: role='deletion' / 'insertion' could be added for SR.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Char-level reveals exact change. 2) Strikethrough for removed (visual + semantic). 3) Inline single column for compact comparison.

title: Gmail-style g+key shortcuts as chord
design_brief: Building Gmail-style chord shortcuts (g+i for inbox, g+s for starred). Pain point: most apps don't ship chord shortcuts.
ux_reasoning: Press g, then within 800ms press destination key. Visual indicator shows 'g' is armed.
interaction_details: Press destination (i, s, etc.): jumps + disarms.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Gmail/Vim convention. 2) 800ms window. 3) Generic prefix hook.

title: User mention notification with deep-link to context
design_brief: Building mention notifications (think Slack mention, Linear). Pain point: mention notification often loses context.
ux_reasoning: Notification includes: mentioner avatar/name, doc/issue title, snippet of comment. Click: deep-link to exact comment.
interaction_details: Click: deep-link to exact comment in app.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Snippet preview = decision context. 2) Deep link to exact comment. 3) Inline timestamp.

title: Submit button with loading state and minimum show duration
design_brief: Building submit button (think Stripe, Apple Pay). Pain point: button flashes loading state for 100ms.
ux_reasoning: Click submit: show 'Submitting...' state for MIN 600ms even if response faster, so user perceives action.
interaction_details: Even if response in 50ms, still shows loading.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Minimum show duration — no flash. 2) Disabled during load. 3) aria-busy.

title: Area chart with target line and shading above/below
design_brief: Building goal-tracking area chart (think fitness app, sales target). Pain point: goal line often added as afterthought.
ux_reasoning: Area under curve. Horizontal target line. Area ABOVE target shaded green; below shaded red.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Goal line first-class. 2) Above/below shading via clipPath. 3) Goal value labeled inline.

title: Inline data import preview before commit
design_brief: Building CSV/data import (think Airtable, Notion). Pain point: import commits without preview.
ux_reasoning: After upload, show preview of first 10 rows with column mapping dropdowns. Commit explicit.
interaction_details: Each source column maps to target field via dropdown.
Commit button shows total row count.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Preview before commit. 2) Auto column mapping. 3) Explicit commit button.

title: Sticky form section headers during scroll
design_brief: Building long form with sections (think Stripe Atlas, complex signup). Pain point: section context lost when scrolling deep.
ux_reasoning: Each form section has sticky header showing section name + progress within. Stacks during scroll.
interaction_details: Section header sticks at top when scrolling within section.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) CSS sticky — no JS scroll listener. 2) Context preserved deep. 3) Stack naturally.

title: Pannable infinite canvas with grid background
design_brief: Building infinite canvas (think Miro, FigJam). Pain point: most clones break at zoom-in beyond 200%.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Zoom that snaps to integer factors
ux_reasoning: CSS transform on a content layer. Grid drawn via SVG pattern. Pan via drag; zoom via scroll wheel with smooth lerp.
interaction_details: Content layer transforms; grid stays sharp via background-size.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) CSS transforms (perf). 2) Grid via radial-gradient bg-size scaled. 3) Min 0.1 / max 5x zoom.

title: App shell with FAB on mobile, toolbar on desktop
design_brief: Building primary creation affordance (think Gmail compose, Trello add). Pain point: same UI everywhere.
ux_reasoning: Mobile: floating action button (FAB) bottom-right. Desktop: top toolbar with same actions.
interaction_details: Mobile: single FAB for primary action.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Material Design FAB on mobile. 2) Desktop toolbar with all actions. 3) Both share data.

title: Tooltip that doesn't trap focus
design_brief: Building tooltip primitive (think Radix UI). Pain point: tooltips that trap focus or fail to dismiss on Esc.
ux_reasoning: Tooltip uses aria-describedby on trigger. Esc dismisses. Does NOT steal focus. Hover or focus shows.
interaction_details: Mouse-out OR blur OR Esc: hide.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Esc dismissal. 2) Focus-trigger (keyboard support). 3) Doesn't steal focus.

title: Background image with low-quality placeholder
design_brief: Building hero with bg-image (think landing pages, photo blogs). Pain point: hero is blank until image loads.
ux_reasoning: Inline base64 8x8 LQIP as bg-image. Real image loaded by JS, swaps on load. Same aspect ratio prevents shift.
accessibility_considerations: Hero text in z-10 layer accessible.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) base64 LQIP — no extra network request. 2) Aspect-ratio container. 3) Crossfade 200ms.

title: Brutalist quotes landing — bold serif and color blocks
design_brief: Building marketing 'testimonial' section in brutalist style (think SSENSE editorial). Pain point: generic quote cards.
ux_reasoning: Large serif quote on plain bg. Attribution in caps tracking-widest underneath, smaller. Quote mark as oversized typographic element.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Oversized quote mark as design element. 2) Serif voice for testimonial. 3) Attribution as editorial caption.

title: Compact data card with stat + trend mini-chart
design_brief: Building stat card (think Stripe dashboard tile). Pain point: most cards waste space.
ux_reasoning: Card: label top + stat value + delta + 80x40 trend sparkline. Compact.
interaction_details: Label / value / delta / trend in compact 224x96 card.
Delta color: green up / red down.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Three signals (value, delta, trend) in one tile. 2) Sparkline at bottom, not separate. 3) Compact 224px width.

title: Number input arrow-keys for ± step
design_brief: Building stepper input (think Stripe price input). Pain point: typing only, no keyboard increment.
ux_reasoning: Arrow up/down: ±1. Shift+arrow: ±10. Cmd+arrow: ±100. Standard convention.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Three step sizes via modifiers. 2) Min/max clamp. 3) Mono tabular for stable width.

title: Live cursor count badge on document tab
design_brief: Building tab-level presence indicator (think Notion sidebar). Pain point: navigating tree, hard to see who's where.
ux_reasoning: Each tab/page in sidebar shows small avatar dot if others present. Hover for names.
interaction_details: Tab/page in sidebar shows colored dots if others viewing.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Per-page presence visible without clicking. 2) Color dots = per-user identity. 3) Hover for full names.

title: Inline edit with cmd+enter to save, esc to cancel
design_brief: Building inline edit pattern used everywhere (titles, descriptions). Pain point: inconsistent commit shortcuts.
ux_reasoning: Click text: becomes editable. Cmd+Enter saves; Esc cancels. Blur saves (with debounce). Always consistent.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Cmd+Enter convention. 2) Esc reverts (not just closes). 3) Blur saves (forgiving).

title: Annotation timeline below time-series chart
design_brief: Building event-annotated chart (think Datadog deploy markers). Pain point: events on chart cluttered or hidden.
ux_reasoning: Chart on top; thin 16px annotation track below with colored markers for events. Hover marker for detail.
interaction_details: 16px annotation track below with colored markers.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Separate annotation track. 2) Color = event kind. 3) Hover for detail.

title: Daily prompt as ambient suggestion
design_brief: Building habit-app prompt (think Things 3 Today, Apple Health). Pain point: nag notifications.
ux_reasoning: On daily app open, ambient banner: 'Today's focus: ___'. Tap engages; ignore to use app normally.
interaction_details: Banner once per day at app open.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Once-per-day enforcement via localStorage. 2) Inline banner, no notification. 3) Engage or dismiss.

title: Multi-tag input with paste-multiple and dedupe
design_brief: Building email recipients input (think email To: field). Pain point: paste of comma-separated emails fails.
ux_reasoning: Paste emails separated by comma OR semicolon OR newline: auto-split + dedupe. Validate format.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Multi-separator support. 2) Paste splits. 3) Dedupe. 4) Validation per chip.

title: Annotation hotspots on 3D scene with leader lines
design_brief: Building 3D scene with text annotations (think NASA mission viewer). Pain point: annotations overlap or detach from anchors.
ux_reasoning: Each annotation has 3D anchor point. HTML overlay positioned via projection. Leader line connects label to anchor.

design_tokens.colors: Anchor dot: oklch(0.55 0.18 250). Leader line: same.

what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Leader line from 3D anchor to 2D label. 2) Uses react-three-drei Html. 3) Real DOM text.

title: Form section: card on mobile, inline on desktop
design_brief: Building form sections (think Stripe Connect). Pain point: cards on desktop feel disconnected; flat on mobile is cramped.
ux_reasoning: Mobile: each section a separate card. Desktop: flat sections divided by borders.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Cards on mobile (visual grouping). 2) Flat on desktop (lighter). 3) Same heading semantic.

title: Forced colors mode tested styling
design_brief: Building UI that works in Windows High Contrast / forced-colors. Pain point: most UIs break completely.
ux_reasoning: Use system colors (CanvasText, Canvas, ButtonText, etc.) via @media (forced-colors: active). Real borders not shadows. SVG icons with currentColor.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) System color tokens. 2) Border, not shadow. 3) SVG via currentColor.

title: Image set with proper srcset breakpoints
design_brief: Building responsive images with multiple sources (think Next.js Image, Cloudinary). Pain point: serving desktop-size to mobile = wasteful.
ux_reasoning: srcset with image widths matching common viewport sizes + 2x DPR. sizes attribute tells browser which to pick.
interaction_details: sizes prop tells browser size at viewport.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Standard 6-width set. 2) sizes per viewport. 3) Browser-native selection.

title: Mid-century modern with warm beige and orange accents
design_brief: Building a furniture / lifestyle brand site (think Joybird, Article). Pain point: most clones use cool neutrals.
ux_reasoning: Warm cream bg. Burnt orange + sage green accents. Geometric sans-serif headlines (Futura). Atomic-age circle motifs.

design_tokens.colors: Bg: oklch(0.95 0.04 80). Ink: oklch(0.18 0.005 30). Accent orange: oklch(0.65 0.18 50). Sage: oklch(0.65 0.06 145).

interaction_details: Burnt orange + sage circles as decoration.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Period-correct warm palette. 2) Atomic-age circles. 3) Round product image (rare).

title: Search-as-you-type with highlighted matches
design_brief: Building search with highlights (think docs search, Algolia). Pain point: matches hard to see.
ux_reasoning: Search results show matched substring with <mark> tag — bg highlighted. Title + path both highlighted.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Real <mark> semantic. 2) Both fields. 3) Case-insensitive.

title: Modal with Tab cycle within and Esc dismiss
design_brief: Building accessible modal (think Radix Dialog). Pain point: Tab escapes modal or doesn't cycle.
ux_reasoning: Tab cycles between focusable inside modal. Esc closes + returns focus.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Real focus trap. 2) Focus restoration. 3) Portal-rendered.

title: Editing user lock with timeout
design_brief: Building editing lock (think Excel co-edit lock, but smart). Pain point: locks forgotten if user closes tab.
ux_reasoning: User editing claims a lock. Lock expires after 60s if no heartbeat. Other users see 'X is editing' with countdown.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Heartbeat-based lock expiry. 2) Live countdown. 3) Auto-clears (no zombie locks).

title: Animated progress ring with completion celebration
design_brief: Building circular progress (think Apple Activity rings). Pain point: most rings just fill silently.
ux_reasoning: Ring fills 0-100% via stroke-dashoffset. At 100%: brief glow pulse + filled state.

design_tokens.colors: Stroke: brand. Glow: brand at low alpha.

what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) stroke-dashoffset smooth fill. 2) Completion glow only at 100%. 3) Live percentage inside.

title: Stacked horizontal progress (multi-segment goals)
design_brief: Building multi-segment progress (think Apple Fitness, budget split). Pain point: stack lost in linear bar.
ux_reasoning: Horizontal bar split into colored segments per category. Each segment shows label + value on hover.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Multi-segment stacked. 2) Hover-isolate via legend opacity. 3) Compact, scannable.

title: Magic-link sign-in with seamless redirect
design_brief: Building magic-link auth (think Notion, Linear). Pain point: clicking link opens new tab, original tab orphaned.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Cold link landing with no progress
ux_reasoning: Email click → opens in any tab. Both tabs (link tab + waiting tab) sync via BroadcastChannel. Link tab handles auth + closes. Waiting tab proceeds.
interaction_details: User clicks email link in any tab.
Link tab verifies + posts message + closes.
Original waiting tab receives event + proceeds.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Cross-tab sync via BroadcastChannel. 2) Original tab proceeds automatically. 3) Link tab auto-closes.
